candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; equals Net sales in preview and is an explicit total revenue line.
Not revenue; this is cost line, so not appropriate except as false candidate.
Operating expense line, not revenue.
Balance sheet liability, not top-line revenue.
Also a valid top-line revenue label; identical to Total Revenue in preview and useful as alternate over time.

variables.notes: Selected revenue labels as priority list per rule; both are valid and identical in preview.

candidates.why: Direct COGS label with populated values.

candidates.row_label: Cost of goods and services sold
candidates.why: Semantically valid COGS label, but appears unpopulated in preview; keep as alternate in case used in other years.
Explicit COGS line and populated; duplicate concept to Cost of goods sold/services.

variables.notes: Chose the most direct populated COGS label. Did not include duplicate alternative lines to keep final choice minimal.

candidates.why: Potential SG&A component, but appears empty in preview.
Could be overhead component, but may also include non-SG&A items such as impairments and miscellaneous operating items.
Depreciation-related and explicitly excluded from XSGA.
Amortization-related and explicitly excluded from XSGA.
Depreciation-related and explicitly excluded from XSGA.

variables.notes: Candidate list clearly missed the strong SG&A label 'Administrative costs' present in the A-column. No explicit standalone SG&A total row exists apart from 'Sales, administration and R&D expenses', which mixes SG&A with R&D and is therefore not suitable for XSGA alone. Included 'Administrative costs' as core overhead, plus 'Other operating expenses' and 'Selling Expenses' as possible overhead buckets. Manual review recommended because 'Other operating expenses' may contain some non-SG&A items in certain years. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Administrative costs

candidates.why: Contains R&D but is a mixed SG&A+R&D total, so less preferred than a dedicated R&D line.
Direct dedicated R&D expense line with populated values.
Amortization component, not the core R&D expense line.
Depreciation component, not the core R&D expense line.
Also a dedicated R&D label but appears empty in preview; valid alternate.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental amortization line, not core current-period R&D expense.
Impairment, not core R&D expense.
Balance sheet asset, not income statement R&D expense.
Balance sheet gross asset, not R&D expense.
Balance sheet accumulated amortization, not current R&D expense.

variables.notes: Selected dedicated R&D labels as priority list; avoided mixed operating-expense subtotal and supplemental/amortization/impairment lines.

candidates.why: Best direct interest expense line with populated values.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest-related but narrower lease-specific component; useful only as fallback priority item.
Interest-related alternate line for some years.
Generic ideal label, but appears empty in preview; keep as alternate over time.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental lease-interest line, narrower and less preferred.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Priority list for interest expense over time; starts with the most direct borrowings interest line and then broader/alternate interest labels.

candidates.row_label: Shareholders' equity attributable to the parent company's owners
candidates.why: Preferred owner/parent equity line that excludes non-controlling interests.
Includes non-controlling interests, so less preferred when parent-only equity exists.
Balancing row, not appropriate when real equity lines exist.

final_choice.row_label: Shareholders' equity attributable to the parent company's owners

variables.notes: Preferred parent-owner equity line to avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, but balance sheet NCI is preferred for MIB/book equity denominator.
Income statement attribution line, less preferred than balance sheet NCI.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive income attributable to noncontrolling interests
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attribution, not balance sheet minority interest.
Direct balance sheet NCI line; preferred for MIB.
Alternate balance sheet NCI label; useful as fallback over time.

variables.notes: Selected balance sheet NCI labels as priority list; avoids using income statement attribution lines.
